rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Release Of Property From Lien Or For Judgment That No Lien Exists, And For Costs And Attorney'S Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 28, 2022 line 1. PETITIONER PARAMOUNT ESTATE LLC'S MOTION / NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION FOR RELEASE OF PROPERTY FROM LIEN OR FOR JUDGMENT THAT NO LIEN EXISTS, AND FOR COSTS AND ATTORNEY'S FEES is OFF CALENDAR. No proof of service on file. Moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DORAN MCGEE ET AL VS. BROWN FAMILY TRUST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Doran Mcgee'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Chieh-Ping Shens Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Number 12.1 And 12.7; Request For Sancts Against Defendant And Her Attorney, Kathryn Klaus, In The Amount Of $1,422
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 28, 2022 line 3. PLAINTIFF DORAN MCGEE'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT CHIEH-PING SHEN'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, NUMBER 12.1 AND 12.7; REQUEST FOR SANCTS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HER ATTORNEY, KATHRYN KLAUS, IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,422 is GRANTED. Defendant Shen shall provide further code-compliant responses without objection and pay sanctions in the amount of $1,422 within 20 days of the date of order. Defendant Shen's Preliminary Statement is stricken because it is not a permissible mode of response under Code of Civil Procedure Section 2030.210. As to form interrogatory 12.1, the defendant's objections to the definition of "INCIDENT" as vague and ambiguous are overruled, said definition having been provided by the Judicial Council. The defendant's remaining objections are stricken as unsupported boilerplate. The defendant's response based on her lack of knowledge is not full and complete as required to the extent it addresses the defendant's knowledge as of the time of purchase of the subject property instead of the time of response. Defendant's further representations of her lack of knowledge are contradicted by her supposition that her husband may have relevant information of which she was required to make a good faith inquiry. As to form interrogatory 12.7, the same rulings apply to the defendant's objections. Defendant's response as to the facts of any inspection, "In connection with the issues in this case, not to date", is ambiguous and incomplete on its face as well as inconsistent with the defendant's acknowledgment that she was in fact involved in inspections of the subject property. The response concerning the latter inspections is incomplete in failing to identify all individuals involved and in failing to state the approximate dates of inspection if the exact dates are unknown. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
